mapping.a_element: microscopic derivation of a master equation for a quantum system (Jaynes-Cummings model with cavity losses)
mapping.b_element: reducible representations of CAR/CCR for field quantization (Dirac/Maxwell fields)

mapping.a_element: dissipator terms varying with vacuum Rabi frequency
mapping.b_element: field operators as operators (not operator-valued distributions)

mapping.a_element: Rotating Wave Approximation (RWA) as a limiting case
mapping.b_element: formalism leading to finite quantum field theory (as a limiting or special case)

brief_justification: While both papers address quantum systems and their mathematical descriptions, Paper A focuses on a microscopic derivation of a master equation for dissipation in a specific Hamiltonian system, whereas Paper B deals with reducible representations of canonical (anti)commutation relations to achieve well-defined field operators. The core mechanisms—dissipative dynamics via master equations vs. algebraic representations for field quantization—are fundamentally distinct.
